name: jing-ye-si
title: 靜夜思 (Jìng Yè Sī) — Quiet Night Thoughts
author: 李白 (Li Bai)
line: 床前明月光，疑是地上霜。
gloss: Before my bed, bright moonlight — I take it for frost on the ground.
imagery: A still dark room, one pale pool of cold moonlight spilled across the floor so even it reads as frost. Maximal 留白; near-total stillness with a single slow drift.
motion: The luminous parallelogram of moonlight translates and elongates almost imperceptibly across the lower screen as the moon moves; its feathered edge shimmers faintly via fBm, and sparse frost-glints twinkle in and out inside the lit patch.
concept: Draw a soft-edged luminous parallelogram low on screen, edges feathered with fBm noise, slowly shearing and translating over a multi-minute cycle. Scatter ~8-15 tiny additive frost points inside it, each fading in/out on a per-point phase-offset sine. Palette: near-black indigo #08091a background, cold moonlight #cfe0ff fill, faint silver #e8f0ff speckle. Most of the screen stays dark and clean for text.

name: chun-jiang-hua-yue-ye
title: 春江花月夜 (Chūn Jiāng Huā Yuè Yè) — Spring River, Flower, Moon, Night
author: 張若虛 (Zhang Ruoxu)
line: 灩灩隨波千萬里。
gloss: Its glittering light follows the waves for ten thousand li.
imagery: A huge round moon lifts off a calm sea-river; its reflection scatters into a long shimmering road of light running across countless swells toward the viewer — a million moonglints riding ripples.
motion: The moon disc eases slowly upward from the bottom edge; its specular 'glitter road' continuously sparkles and breaks apart as wave crests pass — a shifting field of highlights traveling outward, dense near the moon and thinning with distance.
concept: Render a bright soft moon disc easing upward in a narrow vertical strip. Below it compute specular highlights from a moving wave-normal noise field, gating brightness above a threshold so glints pop and vanish; density falls off with distance from the disc. Palette: black-violet sky #0b0a1e, silver-white #eef4ff sparkles, faint warm horizon line. Wide dark margins left and right for text.

name: ba-jiu-wen-yue
title: 把酒問月 (Bǎ Jiǔ Wèn Yuè) — Asking the Moon, Cup in Hand
author: 李白 (Li Bai)
line: 皎如飛鏡臨丹闕，綠煙滅盡清輝發。
gloss: Bright as a flying mirror it nears the vermilion gate; the green mist burns away and pure radiance pours forth.
imagery: A mirror-bright moon partly veiled by low green-grey mist. The drama is the mist thinning and 'burning away' to release a sudden flood of clear silver light, then re-gathering.
motion: On a slow breathing cycle a band of jade-green fog thins to near-nothing; as it clears the moon's bloom and a faint volumetric ray-fan swell to peak brightness (清輝發), then the fog slowly re-gathers and dims the disc.
concept: Draw a mirror-bright moon top-center with a soft bloom halo. Overlay drifting green-grey fBm fog whose density is driven by a slow sine; couple inverse fog-density to bloom radius + a subtle additive ray-fan. Palette: deep blue-black #060a16, faint jade mist #4a6b5a, brilliant cool-white moon #f2f6ff. Lower screen kept dark and open for text.

name: jiang-xue
title: 江雪 (Jiāng Xuě) — River Snow
author: 柳宗元 (Liu Zongyuan)
line: 孤舟蓑笠翁，獨釣寒江雪。
gloss: A lone boat, a straw-cloaked old man, fishing alone in the cold river snow.
imagery: Total white emptiness over a black river: snow falling everywhere, all peaks and paths erased, one tiny boat with a solitary figure at the dead center of the void. The poem itself is 留白.
motion: Sparse, slow snow grains drift straight down with tiny horizontal jitter across the whole frame; the lone boat rocks 2-3px almost imperceptibly; a single hairline glow (the fishing line) hangs down and micro-oscillates.
concept: Near-black field with low-density cool-grey snow particles falling slowly, slight lateral wander. One dim warm-amber point low-center (boat/figure) with a slow sway, plus a faint vertical hairline glow beneath it. Palette: ink-black #05060a → cold pewter #b6cff5 → one amber ember #ffbc6b. Flake density deliberately thin so glyphs sit in the empty dark.

name: ye-xue
title: 夜雪 (Yè Xuě) — Night Snow
author: 白居易 (Bai Juyi)
line: 夜深知雪重，時聞折竹聲。
gloss: Deep in the night I know the snow is heavy — now and then I hear bamboo snap.
imagery: A dark room mysteriously brightened from outside by accumulated snow-glow through a paper window. No falling snow is seen — only the cold luminous wash, and the occasional unseen SNAP of bamboo.
motion: A long, almost imperceptible swelling of the window glow = snow deepening. On a long random timer a faint vertical bamboo silhouette bows, then releases with a brief bright ripple/shudder (the 折竹聲 made visual). Otherwise stillness.
concept: Near-black room. A soft cool-white rounded rectangle (paper window) whose brightness ramps up slowly over a multi-minute cycle. At one edge, faint jade bamboo lines; one bows on a slow curve then snaps back with a short bright shudder on a randomized timer. Palette: black #04050a → moon-white window #e6eeff → faint jade-green #3a5a48 edge. Extreme 留白.

name: bai-xue-ge
title: 白雪歌送武判官歸京 (Báixuě Gē) — Song of White Snow, Seeing Off Adjutant Wu
author: 岑參 (Cen Shen)
line: 忽如一夜春風來，千樹萬樹梨花開。
gloss: As if overnight a spring wind came, and ten-thousand pear trees burst into blossom.
imagery: Frontier dawn: bare trees suddenly laden with snow, every branch crusted white like an explosion of pear blossom — blooms of brightness opening one after another against a dark sky.
motion: Procedural 'blossom' points ignite open in staggered bursts along faint diagonal branch curves, each blooming soft-white with a cool halo then settling; a low wind shears the whole field so blossoms quiver and a few stray flakes peel off and drift down.
concept: Dark indigo dawn ground. Place bloom nodes along a few faint diagonal branch curves; each node scales a feathered white disk open on a staggered timer, with a cool halo. Apply slow wind-shear displacement to all nodes; spawn occasional falling flake particles. Palette: deep indigo #0a1024 → silver-white #f4f8ff → faint blush #f6c5be. Heavy negative space between branch arcs.

name: feng-xue-su
title: 逢雪宿芙蓉山主人 (Féng Xuě Sù Fúróngshān) — Caught in Snow, Lodging at Lotus Mountain
author: 劉長卿 (Liu Changqing)
line: 柴門聞犬吠，風雪夜歸人。
gloss: At the brushwood gate a dog barks — through wind and snow, someone comes home in the night.
imagery: Night blizzard at a remote cottage. One warm lit lantern at a brushwood gate; everything else is dark wind-driven snow. A faint figure approaches the single point of warmth.
motion: Wind-driven snow streaks diagonally at ~30°, gusting in sinusoidal density waves (a real gale, not vertical drift). The lantern sways and its glow pulses with each gust; a dim dark-warm blob eases slowly inward from the frame edge toward the light.
concept: Black ground, cold-blue snow streaks raked at ~30° with a sinusoidal wind-envelope modulating density and angle. One warm-amber lantern node low and off-center with a soft pulsing bloom the streaks scatter through. A faint dark-warm blob drifts in from the edge. Palette: night-black #05060d → steel-blue snow #9fb6d8 → amber lantern #ffae57. 留白 in the upper dark sky for text.

name: wang-lushan-pubu
title: 望廬山瀑布 (Wàng Lúshān Pùbù) — Gazing at the Lushan Waterfall
author: 李白 (Li Bai)
line: 飛流直下三千尺，疑是銀河落九天。
gloss: The torrent plunges straight down three thousand feet — as if the Milky Way were falling from heaven.
imagery: A single vertical ribbon of white water plummeting down a black mountain face, purple sun-mist glowing at the summit, the fall so long it reads as the galaxy poured to earth.
motion: The water column streams continuously DOWNWARD (downward-scrolling fBm); fine spray disperses and drifts as particles at the base; the purple incense-burner haze rises slowly at the top in slow counter-motion.
concept: One bright vertical band of downward-scrolling fBM on a near-black field, edges feathered into rising spray particles at the base. Top: a slow purple-magenta glow drifting upward. Palette: cold silver-white #dfe9ff water core, violet mist #8a63ce, deep charcoal #07080e background. Heavy 留白 left and right of the single fall.

name: lang-tao-sha
title: 浪淘沙·其七 (Làng Táo Shā · Qī) — Waves Scouring the Sand, No. 7
author: 劉禹錫 (Liu Yuxi)
line: 八月濤聲吼地來，捲起沙堆似雪堆。
gloss: August's tidal roar comes bellowing over the land, rolling up sand-heaps like drifts of snow.
imagery: The Qiantang tidal bore: a single towering wall of white water surging in across a dark estuary, rearing and breaking, then sucking back out — churned foam piled white like snow.
motion: A massive foam-crest line advances fast across the frame, rears and peaks, then recedes — a surge-and-retreat loop; spray particles fling off the crest at the top of the surge; foam streaks roll along the shore.
concept: Dark sea plane with one tall advancing foam-crest band (bright turbulent noise) whose vertical position and height follow a surge-and-retreat envelope on a loop. At peak, emit spray particles off the crest. Palette: white-and-pale-cyan foam #d8f4ff, dark teal sea #0a2630, near-black sky #04080b. Open dark sky above the wave is the text space.

name: yin-hu-shang
title: 飲湖上初晴後雨 (Yǐn Húshàng) — Drinking on the Lake, First Clear Then Rain
author: 蘇軾 (Su Shi)
line: 水光潋灩晴方好，山色空濛雨亦奇。
gloss: Rippling water glittering — fine in clear weather; hazy mountains dim in rain — wondrous too.
imagery: West Lake in two moods at once: sun-glittered ripples shimmering on the foreground water, and soft veils of rain-mist blurring the far hills into emptiness.
motion: Traveling micro-highlights ripple across the lake (animated specular sparkle following a ripple field); a curtain of fine rain drifts diagonally; the far-hill mist slowly breathes, dissolving and reforming.
concept: A dark lake plane with traveling additive specular glints driven by a moving ripple-normal field in the lower foreground; a soft drifting rain-mist gradient softening a faint hill silhouette behind, its density on a slow sine. Sparse diagonal rain streaks. Palette: jade-teal water #11403c, silver glints #eaf6ff, pale grey mist #aab6bf. Foreground sparkle low, misty 留白 up top.

name: chun-xiao
title: 春曉 (Chūn Xiǎo) — Spring Dawn
author: 孟浩然 (Meng Haoran)
line: 夜來風雨聲，花落知多少。
gloss: Through the night, wind and rain — how many blossoms have fallen?
imagery: Pre-dawn dimness after a night storm. Petals loosened, drifting down through still air. No people, just the quiet aftermath — a few last petals released, sparse and slow.
motion: Individual petals detach and fall on gentle sinusoidal sway paths (the storm already past, so the descent is soft and residual); each fades in at the top and out near the bottom. Very low density, never crowding.
concept: 5-8 soft pink-white petal glyphs at most on screen, drifting down along faint sine sway paths against near-black, with rotation and fade in/out. Palette: warm petal-pink #f6c5be and pale cream #fef1d1 on charcoal #0a0a10. Heavy negative space — petals occupy under 10% of cells.

name: shan-xing
title: 山行 (Shān Xíng) — Mountain Travel
author: 杜牧 (Du Mu)
line: 停車坐愛楓林晚，霜葉紅於二月花。
gloss: I stop the cart, in love with the maple woods at dusk — frosted leaves redder than spring blossoms.
imagery: A hillside of maples at evening, leaves blazing crimson under frost, loosening and tumbling down the slope. Warm red against cold blue dusk and dark stone path.
motion: Maple-leaf glyphs spin and tumble as they fall — rotating, catching gusts, fluttering on a slightly chaotic diagonal descent (heavier, more tumbling than gentle petals).
concept: Sparse crimson/orange leaf glyphs tumble diagonally from top-right to bottom-left with per-leaf rotation and small gust jitter, against deep indigo-black. A faint warm gradient glow along the bottom edge (the maple wood). Palette: maple red #cc3a21, ember orange #eaa041, indigo #0a0d1e ground. Low density, large open center for text.

name: yong-liu
title: 詠柳 (Yǒng Liǔ) — Ode to the Willow
author: 賀知章 (He Zhizhang)
line: 碧玉妝成一樹高，萬條垂下綠絲絛。
gloss: Dressed in jade, the tall tree stands; ten thousand strands hang down like green silk ribbons.
imagery: A tall willow in early spring, countless slender green strands hanging and trailing, swaying as one in a light wind — vertical drapes of green silk catching the breeze.
motion: Thin vertical willow-strand lines sway side to side in a slow wind wave — a phase-shifted oscillation propagating left-to-right across the curtain of strands, like silk catching a gust.
concept: Sparse thin vertical chains hang from the top edge, each swaying with a sine whose phase offset increases across X so a gust visibly travels rightward. Keep strands thin and well-spaced. Palette: jade green #42d692 + pale willow #a0eac9 on dark teal-black #04120f. Strands anchored at top so the lower two-thirds stays open for text.

name: qian-tang-hu-chun-xing
title: 錢塘湖春行 (Qiántánghú Chūn Xíng) — Spring Stroll by Qiantang Lake
author: 白居易 (Bai Juyi)
line: 幾處早鶯爭暖樹，誰家新燕啄春泥。
gloss: Early orioles vie for sun-warmed trees; whose new swallows peck at spring mud?
imagery: Early spring by West Lake: calm bright water, a low warm sun, and swallows darting and swooping low over the surface. Fresh, open, luminous.
motion: 2-3 swallow glyphs trace fast curved bezier swoop arcs across a wide dark field, dipping toward a faint lower water line and rising again — quick darting flight against stillness is the whole subject.
concept: 2-3 forked swallow glyphs follow fast bezier swoop paths across a wide dark field, dipping near a single dim horizontal shimmer line (the lake) then climbing. Palette: luminous teal-white birds #98d7e4 on deep blue-black #060a18, one faint water glint. Almost no static elements, so text stays clear.

name: feng-qiao-ye-bo
title: 楓橋夜泊 (Fēngqiáo Yè Bó) — Night Mooring at Maple Bridge
author: 張繼 (Zhang Ji)
line: 月落烏啼霜滿天，江楓漁火對愁眠。
gloss: The moon sets, a crow cries, frost fills the sky; riverside maples and fishing-fires keep watch over sleepless grief.
imagery: A cold night harbor: the moon just dropped low, frost-mist in the air, scattered orange fishing-lamps glowing on moored boats across the dark river, doubled in wavering reflections.
motion: Two or three warm fishing-fire points flicker and sway gently; each casts a vertical wavering reflection that ripples and stretches on the rippling river; a thin frost-haze band drifts slowly across the upper sky; a pale moon eases downward at one edge.
concept: Near-black river-night. 2-3 warm flickering point-lights low on screen, each with a sine-distorted vertical reflection streak beneath it on a UV ripple field. A thin horizontal frost-fog band scrolls slowly above; a dim moon disc sinks at one edge. Palette: ember-orange fires #ffae57, cold moon-silver #cfe0ff, deep teal-black #04100f. Large dark center for text.

name: shi-zhi-sai-shang
title: 使至塞上 (Shǐ Zhì Sàishàng) — On a Mission to the Frontier
author: 王維 (Wang Wei)
line: 大漠孤煙直，長河落日圓。
gloss: Over the vast desert a lone smoke-column rises straight; on the long river the setting sun hangs round.
imagery: An immense flat desert at dusk reduced to two pure forms: a single perfectly vertical thread of beacon-smoke rising into windless sky, and a huge round low sun resting on the horizon above a wide river.
motion: The beacon smoke drifts upward as a slow near-vertical luminous filament with a slight heat-haze wobble; the round sun sinks almost imperceptibly toward the horizon; a faint warm shimmer ripples across the river band beneath it.
concept: Near-black desert ground meets deep-indigo sky. A single rising vertical noise-displaced smoke ribbon (amber→ash) on the left third; a large soft-edged orange disc low-right descending slowly, its reflection a horizontal shimmer band. Maximal 留白 — two glowing primitives on emptiness. Palette: ember orange #ff8c42, dusty gold #d5ae49, deep indigo #0a1024, charcoal #06070c.

name: deng-guanque-lou
title: 登鸛雀樓 (Dēng Guànquè Lóu) — Climbing Stork Tower
author: 王之渙 (Wáng Zhīhuàn)
line: 白日依山盡，黃河入海流。
gloss: The white sun leans on the mountains and is spent; the Yellow River pours on into the sea.
imagery: A vast luminous panorama: a pale sun sinking onto distant ranges on one side, and the great tawny river running diagonally across the plain toward a far sea on the other. Height, distance, and a river that never stops moving outward — boundless horizontal sweep.
motion: A broad ochre river-band flows steadily DIAGONALLY across the frame toward the far horizon (continuous downstream UV scroll with bright current-glints traveling along it), thinning into a sea-haze at the vanishing point; simultaneously the round white sun eases almost imperceptibly downward onto the mountain line at the opposite edge. The river's relentless outward flow is the main motion.
concept: A wide low horizon. From upper-left to lower-right, a tapering ochre river-band carries scrolling current-glint highlights (threshold-gated specular on a flow field) that travel toward a pale sea-glow at the horizon. Upper-right: a soft white-gold sun disc resting on a dark mountain silhouette, sinking on a multi-minute ramp. Palette: dusty gold river #d5ae49, pale sea-haze #c9daf8, white-gold sun #fef1d1, indigo dusk sky #0a1024, dark ranges #06070c. The high open sky above the river is reserved for text. (Differs from shi-zhi-sai-shang: there the sun is static on a still river with a vertical smoke thread; here the signature motion is a continuously FLOWING diagonal river running to a sea-haze vanishing point — a moving-water scene, not a still-desert one.)

name: song-meng-haoran
title: 送孟浩然之廣陵 (Sòng Mèng Hàorán zhī Guǎnglíng) — Seeing Off Meng Haoran for Guangling
author: 李白 (Lǐ Bái)
line: 孤帆遠影碧空盡，唯見長江天際流。
gloss: The lone sail's far shadow vanishes into the blue void; only the long river is seen, flowing to the edge of the sky.
imagery: A single tiny sail shrinking against an enormous empty bright sky over open water; the river widens toward the viewer and narrows to nothing at the sky-line. Almost the entire frame is luminous void — the perfect 留白 — with one speck of a boat.
motion: The lone sail RECEDES — it drifts away from the viewer and downstream, growing smaller and fainter until it dissolves at the horizon; the river surface beneath keeps flowing toward the sky's edge.
concept: A single small sail-triangle that scales down and fades (alpha→0) along a perspective line toward a high vanishing point, then respawns to loop. Underneath, a gently scrolling river plane rendered as faint horizontal shimmer lines that converge at the horizon. Palette: luminous pale-cyan/jade sky-water #c9daf8, one warm-white sail #fef1d1, deep teal-black margins #04100f. The shrinking, vanishing sail is the entire emotional motion — slow and irreversible-feeling. A receding-boat archetype absent from the set.

name: yu-ge-zi
title: 漁歌子 (Yú Gē Zǐ) — Fisherman's Song
author: 張志和 (Zhāng Zhìhé)
line: 西塞山前白鷺飛，桃花流水鱖魚肥。
gloss: Before Xisai Mountain the white egrets fly; peach blossoms drift on the stream where the mandarin fish grow fat.
imagery: A faint mountain silhouette; a white egret crossing in front of it; below, a stream carrying scattered pink peach petals downstream. Wide quiet space above and around.
motion: TWO clear motions: a white egret GLIDES across the frame (a slow arc of wings), and peach petals DRIFT downstream on the current, rotating slightly as they float past. The egret's flight is the lead motion; the petals are the secondary continuous drift.
concept: A single egret rendered as a bright moving glyph/curve that arcs left-to-right with a subtle wing-flap (sine bob), passing in front of a dark distant mountain SDF. Below, a particle stream of small pink petal sprites advected along a flow field. Palette: glowing white egret #f4f8ff, soft blush-pink petals #f6c5be, deep slate-green water #16323c, charcoal sky #06070c. Lots of empty upper space for text; motion confined to a horizontal band low in the frame. Distinct from qian-tang's fast-darting swallows: this is a single slow gliding wader crossing a static mountain — a wholly different bird gait and silhouette.

name: tianjingsha-qiusi
title: 天淨沙·秋思 (Tiānjìngshā · Qiūsī) — Autumn Thoughts (Tune: Sky-Clear Sand)
author: 馬致遠 (Mǎ Zhìyuǎn)
line: 古道西風瘦馬。夕陽西下，斷腸人在天涯。
gloss: On the old road, west wind, a gaunt horse; the evening sun sinks in the west — the heartbroken traveler at the edge of the world.
imagery: A lone silhouetted rider on a thin horse on an empty road, dwarfed by a huge low sunset; bare crow-perched branches at the margin. A vast desolate plain — almost all negative space — under a dying sun.
motion: The setting sun SINKS toward the horizon while the lone horse-and-rider silhouette PLODS slowly across the empty plain (small, left-to-right), the west wind pushing faint dust streaks past them. The plodding silhouette plus the sinking sun together carry the motion.
concept: A large dim blood-orange sun lowering on the right, with a tiny dark horse-rider silhouette translating slowly across a flat horizon line, bobbing with each step (low-frequency vertical wobble). Thin horizontal wind-streaks (fast UV scroll, low alpha) blow across. Palette: muted ember-orange and dusty rose sun #ff8c42/#f6c5be, near-black silhouettes #06070c, vast cold grey-violet plain #4a4660. Maximum 留白; the small moving silhouette draws the eye. The collection has no human/animal figure traversing a landscape and no Yuan-dynasty qu — a unique land-travel archetype and overland silhouette-in-motion.

name: xiao-chi
title: 小池 (Xiǎo Chí) — Small Pond
author: 楊萬里 (Yáng Wànlǐ)
line: 小荷才露尖尖角，早有蜻蜓立上頭。
gloss: The young lotus has just thrust up its sharp little tip — and already a dragonfly has perched upon its head.
imagery: A still, dark pond surface with a single pointed lotus bud emerging; one slender dragonfly hovering and alighting on the bud's tip. Mostly empty water and quiet space around a single delicate focal point.
motion: The dragonfly HOVERS and then lands — darting in small quick movements before settling on the lotus tip, its wings shimmering; faint concentric ripples spread on the pond where the stem meets the water. The dragonfly's hover-and-perch is the obvious thing-in-motion.
concept: A single dragonfly sprite that darts in small jittered steps then eases to rest on a thin lotus-bud spike, wings rendered as fast-flickering translucent quads. Beneath, occasional expanding ring-ripples (animated radius, fading alpha) on a near-black mirror-still pond. Palette: iridescent cyan-green dragonfly #42d692, pale jade lotus tip #a0eac9, glassy black-blue water #04120f. Delicate, small-scale insect motion in one spot; the rest is calm dark water for text. The only insect, the only lotus, and the only fast-jitter-then-settle motion in the set.

name: wang-tianmen-shan
title: 望天門山 (Wàng Tiānménshān) — Gazing at Heaven's Gate Mountains
author: 李白 (Lǐ Bái)
line: 兩岸青山相對出，孤帆一片日邊來。
gloss: From both banks the green peaks come forth to meet me; a lone sail draws near, out of the side of the sun.
imagery: The river has cleaved a mountain in two, forming a gate; as the boat advances, the twin cliffs seem to swing OUT toward the viewer, while far off a single sail emerges from the glare of the sun. Famous for its illusion of mountains 'coming out' because the viewer is moving.
motion: Two facing cliff silhouettes slowly slide APART and grow (scaling/translating outward toward the frame edges) — the 相對出 illusion of peaks advancing as the boat moves through the gate; between them a tiny bright sail glyph drifts INWARD from a glowing sun-disc on the horizon, growing slightly as it nears; the river current shimmers downstream through the gap.
concept: Two dark green-black mountain silhouettes meet near center forming a V-gate; on a slow loop they translate outward and scale up (peaks 'coming forth'), then reset. Through the gap: a small luminous sail point emerges from a warm sun-glow at the horizon and eases inward, scaling up. A narrow river band between the cliffs carries downstream glints. Palette: jade-dark peaks #094228, sun-gold glow #ffad47, white sail #f4f8ff, blue-green water #11403c, deep sky #060a18. The gate opening keeps a clean central column for text. Pairs as a deliberate counter-motion to song-meng-haoran's receding sail (here the sail APPROACHES and the framing cliffs animate) — the only scene with parallax-advancing terrain.

name: mu-jiang-yin
title: 暮江吟 (Mù Jiāng Yín) — Song of the River at Dusk
author: 白居易 (Bái Jūyì)
line: 一道殘陽鋪水中，半江瑟瑟半江紅。
gloss: A last shaft of setting sun spreads across the water; half the river is jade-cold, half blazing red.
imagery: A broad river at sundown. The low sun lays a single rippling band of light flat across the surface; one half of the river still glows red-gold where the light strikes, the other half lies in cold jade-green shadow. The boundary shimmers and shifts as the water moves.
motion: The shimmering, rippling light-band on the water is the mover — a horizontal swath of glints traveling and flickering across the surface (殘陽鋪水中), with the red/jade boundary breathing and the whole band slowly dimming and reddening as the sun sinks. Water ripples animate the glint constantly.
concept: Lower half = river surface, split tonally: a warm side and a cool side meeting at a soft vertical seam mid-frame. A bright horizontal ribbon of sun-glints ripples across (animated noise/sine displacement) and slowly lowers and reddens. Upper half = dim dusk sky, kept empty for text. Palette: blazing amber-red #e8662c on the lit half, deep jade-teal #1e4a44 (the 瑟瑟) on the shadowed half, dark muted sky above. The two-tone split surface is unique — no other scene divides the water into contrasting warm/cool halves, and the dusk-shimmer-on-water archetype is otherwise missing.

name: zhu-li-guan
title: 竹里館 (Zhú Lǐ Guǎn) — Bamboo Lodge
author: 王維 (Wáng Wéi)
line: 深林人不知，明月來相照。
gloss: Deep in the woods no one knows I am here — only the bright moon comes to keep me company, casting its light.
imagery: A lone figure seated in a dense, dark bamboo grove at night. Above and beyond the black columns of bamboo the moon hangs; its silver light slants down through the canes and pools on the ground beside him.
motion: The moonlight 'arrives' — 來相照: a slow shaft of luminance creeping in through the bamboo, brightening as it 'finds' the floor. Secondarily, the faint sway of slender bamboo stalks and the leaf-shadows they throw.
concept: Vertical black bamboo stalks as tall thin negative-space columns down both sides, leaving a clear dark center for text. A volumetric god-ray of pale light sweeps diagonally through the stalks and settles into a soft circular pool near the bottom, brightening on a slow ease curve. Subtle vertical-noise sway warps the bamboo edges. Palette: near-black indigo #08091a, cool moon-silver #cfe3ff for the ray and pool, faint jade rim-light #3a5a48 on bamboo edges. Everything dim except the one moving ray — maximal 留白 in the middle. The light-shaft-through-a-forest archetype (vertical framing columns, a moving god-ray rather than a visible moon disc) appears nowhere else; the moon is implied by its beam, not drawn, so it does not duplicate the four existing moon-disc scenes.

name: chibi-huai-gu
title: 念奴嬌·赤壁懷古 (Niàn Nú Jiāo · Chìbì Huáigǔ) — Charms of Niannu: Meditation on Red Cliff
author: 蘇軾 (Su Shi)
line: 亂石穿空，驚濤拍岸，捲起千堆雪。
gloss: Jagged rocks pierce the sky; startled breakers smash the shore, rolling up a thousand drifts of snow.
imagery: A black rampart of cliff jutting into the night sky; the great river hurling wave after wave against its base, each impact bursting into a high white plume of foam — spray flung up like heaped snow against dark stone. The grandest crashing-wave scene in the canon.
motion: Successive wave-crests sweep IN toward a fixed vertical cliff edge and SLAM, each collision detonating an upward burst of foam particles that arc up, peak, and rain back down; between hits the surge recedes and the foam-line sinks, so the whole frame breathes on a strike-and-retreat pulse. The dominant motion is the repeated explosive impact at the rock face.
concept: Right third: a near-black ragged cliff silhouette with one bright vertical impact edge. A turbulent foam band (fbm gated to the lower-left water region only) advances on a surge envelope; when its leading edge reaches the cliff x, spawn a radial burst of additive spray particles that launch up the rock face, peak, and fall under gravity. Palette: charcoal-black cliff #07080e, deep teal river #0a2630, brilliant white-cyan foam #d8f4ff. Keep the upper-left sky and center wide-open and dark for text. Differs from lang-tao-sha (a horizontal wall of water advancing across open estuary): here the wave IMPACTS a fixed vertical cliff and explodes UPWARD as vertical spray plumes — the only scene with a hard collision against solid rock.

name: zao-fa-baidi
title: 早發白帝城 (Zǎo Fā Báidìchéng) — Leaving Baidi at Dawn
author: 李白 (Lǐ Bái)
line: 兩岸猿聲啼不住，輕舟已過萬重山。
gloss: From both banks the gibbons' cries never cease — yet the light boat has already slipped past ten thousand mountains.
imagery: A tiny skiff shooting downstream through a dawn gorge so fast the cliff-walls on either side stream backward and blur. The thrill is pure velocity: the boat barely moves on screen while the world rushes past it.
motion: Layered gorge-wall silhouettes on the far left and far right edges SCROLL DOWNWARD-and-outward continuously (parallax: near walls fast, far walls slow), so the viewer reads as racing forward. A single small luminous boat glyph holds near center, bobbing 2-3px, while faint horizontal speed-streaks on the river rush past beneath it. The receding mountains are what move.
concept: Two dark layered mountain-edge bands hug the left and right margins; each is a stack of silhouettes scrolling vertically at parallax speeds (foreground fastest) to fake forward rush. Center channel stays open dark sky/water. One small warm-amber boat point holds center with a tiny sway; add a few thin horizontal cyan motion-streaks on the lower river scrolling fast. Palette: dawn-violet sky #1a1530, slate gorge walls #0c1018, amber boat #ffbc6b, pale river streaks #aecbe0. The empty center vertical channel is the text space. The only high-velocity scene and the only parallax-gorge — fills the 'swift river through gorges' gap with motion found nowhere else in the set.

name: qiu-xi
title: 秋夕 (Qiū Xī) — Autumn Night
author: 杜牧 (Dù Mù)
line: 輕羅小扇撲流螢，天階夜色涼如水。
gloss: With a light silk fan she swats at the drifting fireflies; the night on the stone steps is cool as water.
imagery: A cool autumn palace night. Fireflies wander up from the dark grass, their green-gold glow pulsing; a solitary figure with a small silk round-fan idly bats at them. Stone steps lie bathed in a water-cool blue darkness; far above, the paired stars of the Herd-boy and Weaver-girl.
motion: The drifting, pulsing fireflies are the clear thing-in-motion — several soft glowing points wandering on slow looping paths, brightening and dimming (撲流螢). Occasionally one is brushed aside by the fan's arc and darts away. Stars hold steady high above.
concept: Deep indigo-black night, very sparse. 5-8 firefly points wander on lazy Lissajous loops in the lower-middle, each a soft green-gold bloom that breathes (sine-pulsed opacity). A faint pale fan-arc sweeps occasionally and one firefly scatters off it. Top of frame: a scatter of cool pinprick stars, two slightly brighter and paired (牽牛織女). Palette: indigo-black ground #0c0f1a, firefly green-gold #c8e07a→#f0e8a0 glow, stars in cold white-blue #e8f0ff. Enormous still negative space between the low fireflies and the high stars. The only fireflies, the only stars, and the only autumn-night-coolness archetype — luminous green points wandering on loops, distinct from every existing twinkle/glint pattern (which all sit on water).

name: du-zuo-jingting
title: 獨坐敬亭山 (Dú Zuò Jìngtíng Shān) — Sitting Alone by Jingting Mountain
author: 李白 (Lǐ Bái)
line: 眾鳥高飛盡，孤雲獨去閒。
gloss: The flocks of birds have flown high and vanished; a lone cloud drifts away, idle and free.
imagery: A wide, emptying sky over a single quiet mountain. A flock of birds climbs and dwindles to nothing at the top of the sky; then one solitary small cloud floats slowly off and away until the heavens are bare, leaving only the still mountain and the watcher facing it.
motion: A two-stage emptying: first a flock of small bird-marks rising diagonally and shrinking to vanishing (高飛盡), then a single lone cloud drifting laterally out of frame (孤雲獨去). The sky progressively clears — motion that ends in stillness.
concept: A pale graded dusk sky over a low dark mountain silhouette on the bottom edge. Phase 1: ~12 tiny bird glyphs stream upward-right, scaling down to zero opacity. Phase 2 (after they're gone): one soft cloud puff drifts slowly left-to-right and fades out, leaving the sky empty. Loop slowly. Palette: muted dusk wash slate-violet #4a4660 to pale ash, near-black flat mountain mass #06070c, birds and cloud in soft luminous off-white #f4f8ff. The point is the 留白 that remains after everything departs. The only daytime/dusk pale-sky scene, the only drifting cloud, and a unique two-phase 'scene empties to nothing' motion — opposite in feel to every additive-on-black scene.

name: qing-yu-an
title: 青玉案·元夕 (Qīng Yù Àn · Yuán Xī) — The Lantern Festival (Tune: Green Jade Table)
author: 辛棄疾 (Xīn Qìjí)
line: 東風夜放花千樹，更吹落、星如雨。
gloss: The east wind in the night makes a thousand trees burst into flower; it blows them down again — stars falling like rain.
imagery: The single most famous fireworks line in the canon. Over a dark city the night sky erupts: bursts of fire blossom open like a thousand luminous trees (花千樹), then their sparks shower back down through the dark in long glittering streaks (星如雨) — a rain of falling stars made of gunpowder light, drifting down over a dim rooftop skyline.
motion: A two-beat firework cycle is the whole subject: a burst IGNITES (a radial bloom of bright points exploding outward from a point, peaking in ~0.3s), then the sparks PEEL OFF and RAIN DOWN — each arcing downward under gravity, trailing a fading streak and twinkling out before it lands (星如雨). Bursts fire on staggered randomized timers from different upper positions so the falling-star rain is near-continuous; concurrent bursts capped at 1-2 so the center stays open.
concept: Near-black night with a faint dim rooftop/skyline silhouette along the bottom edge for grounding. On staggered randomized timers spawn firework bursts: a radial particle explosion (40-80 additive points launched outward, velocity-scaled, peaking fast) at a random upper position, then let each spark fall under gravity with a fading streaked trail and per-spark twinkle so the bloom decays into a downward rain. Keep concurrent bursts to 1-2 so the middle stays mostly open for text. Palette: deep night sky #060814, gold-white burst cores #fef1d1, warm ember-orange falling sparks fading #ffad47 → #cc3a21, occasional cool-silver bursts #cfe0ff. The dark sky between and after bursts is the text space. The ONLY fireworks/pyrotechnic scene and the only explosion-then-gravity-fall motion in the set — opposite gait, direction, and palette to qiu-xi's slow upward green fireflies.

name: guan-canghai
title: 觀滄海 (Guān Cānghǎi) — Gazing at the Vast Sea
author: 曹操 (Cáo Cāo)
line: 秋風蕭瑟，洪波湧起。日月之行，若出其中。
gloss: The autumn wind sighs and moans; mighty billows surge and rise. The sun and moon in their courses seem to issue from within this sea.
imagery: The poet stands on the rock of Jieshi over an immense open ocean. No near shore-collision, no single wave — the whole sea-plane heaves: long rolling autumn swells lifting and dropping across the entire lower frame to a flat far horizon, the water breathing as one vast body. At the horizon a faint luminous glow sits, the cosmic conceit of sun and moon born out of the sea. Heroic oceanic breadth; the sea itself is the subject, not foam or a boat.
motion: The ENTIRE sea-plane undulates — broad low-frequency swell-lines roll steadily across the frame from horizon toward the viewer, the surface rising and sinking in long heaving ripples (洪波湧起) as a slow autumn-wind envelope swells and eases their amplitude. No impact, no spray: the motion is the massive continuous breathing-and-rolling of deep open water, with faint cool glints sliding along the swell crests. A dim horizon glow (日月之行) pulses almost imperceptibly, as if the light rises from the water.
concept: Lower two-thirds = an open-sea plane built from summed low-frequency sine/fBm swell-bands scrolling toward the viewer in perspective (large wavelength, modest amplitude), shaded so crests catch a cool rim-glint that travels along them. A slow global wind-envelope sine raises and lowers overall swell amplitude (the 秋風蕭瑟 gusting). At the flat horizon seam a faint warm-to-silver glow breathes on a long cycle (sun/moon issuing from the sea). Upper third = bare dark sky for text, heavy 留白. Palette: deep slate-teal sea #0a2630 → #11403c, cold crest-silver glints #c9daf8, faint horizon glow #fef1d1, near-black sky #04080b. The only true blue-water open-ocean SWELL — nothing breaks, distinct from lang-tao-sha's advancing wall and chibi's cliff-slam — and the collection's first Han-dynasty 樂府.

name: teng-wang-ge
title: 滕王閣序 (Téngwáng Gé Xù) — Preface to the Pavilion of Prince Teng
author: 王勃 (Wáng Bó)
line: 落霞與孤鶩齊飛，秋水共長天一色。
gloss: The falling rose-glow and a lone wild duck fly together; the autumn waters merge with the boundless sky into one single color.
imagery: The canonical sunset-with-a-lone-bird vista. A wide luminous dusk where water and sky dissolve into a single seamless wash of rose-gold (共長天一色) — no hard horizon, just a graded field of light. Drifting banners of sunset cloud (落霞) sail slowly across it, and one solitary wild duck (孤鶩) crosses level with them, a small dark-then-luminous silhouette gliding through the glow. Spacious, serene, golden — the most famous 'lone bird at sunset' line in all of Chinese letters.
motion: TWO motions moving in concert (齊飛): long soft rose-gold cloud-bands drift slowly and horizontally across the upper field, AND one lone duck glyph glides across at the same level on a shallow arc with a slow wing-beat bob — bird and cloud travelling together is the whole point. Beneath, the merged water-sky wash shimmers faintly so the seam between water and air never resolves, reinforcing 一色.
concept: A full-frame vertical gradient of rose-gold sunset (no drawn horizon — water and sky share one continuous graded wash, with only a barely-there shimmer band low to hint at water). Across the upper third, 2-3 soft elongated cloud-streaks translate slowly sideways (落霞). At the same altitude ONE small duck silhouette glides across on a gentle bezier with a subtle sine wing-bob, rim-lit by the sun-glow so it reads dark-to-gold; it crosses, fades at the edge, respawns. Faint warm specular shimmer drifts on the lower water wash. Palette: rose-gold #ffad47 / blush #f6c5be high, melting to pale gold-cyan #c9daf8 at the merge zone, one luminous-edged duck #fef1d1→#06070c, dim warm water undertone #7a4706. The open glowing center-left is the text field. Unlike du-zuo-jingting (a pale sky EMPTYING as birds vanish), this stays full and golden with a bird PRESENT and gliding alongside cloud — the only seamless sky-water sunset and the only single-duck-with-cloud archetype.

name: chun-ye-xi-yu
title: 春夜喜雨 (Chūn Yè Xǐ Yǔ) — Delighting in Rain on a Spring Night
author: 杜甫 (Dù Fǔ)
line: 隨風潛入夜，潤物細無聲。
gloss: It steals into the night on the wind, moistening all things finely, without a sound.
imagery: A black spring night. A good rain so fine it is barely rain at all — a soundless, near-invisible mist of micro-droplets riding the wind, settling on unseen buds and earth. Nothing is struck or splashed; the whole field simply grows imperceptibly damp and softly luminous as the moisture beads and catches a faint distant lamp glow. The opposite of a storm: tender, secret, almost weightless precipitation.
motion: The rain DRIFTS rather than falls — a vast field of extremely fine droplets advected slowly downward-and-sideways on a gentle wind (潛入), so slow and diffuse it reads as a breathing mist sliding across the frame rather than streaks. Secondarily, faint dew-beads kindle into existence and softly brighten on unseen surfaces low in the frame (潤物) — points easing up in opacity as the surfaces 'drink' the rain, then holding. The signature is silent near-horizontal infiltration, not vertical rainfall.
concept: Near-black ground. Render thousands of very small low-alpha droplet points advected on a slow leftward-and-down curl-noise wind field, each fading in at one edge and out at the other so the whole sheet appears to seep across the screen. Modulate global density on a long sine so the rain 'breathes in' on the wind. Low in the frame, a sparse scatter of dew-bead points ramp up in brightness on staggered ease curves and hold (things being moistened), giving a faint glistening band. No splash, no impact, no streaking — pure drift. Palette: ink-black ground #060a12, cool faint silver-blue droplets #b6cff5 at very low alpha, soft jade-grey dew sheen #8aa0a8, one barely-warm distant lamp glow #4a4636 at an edge. Almost the entire frame is quiet dark mist — maximal 留白 for text. The only soundless near-horizontal seeping drizzle whose motion is infiltration, not descent — distinct from every existing rain/snow that streaks or splashes with force.

name: jiang-nan-chun
title: 江南春 (Jiāngnán Chūn) — Spring in Jiangnan
author: 杜牧 (Dù Mù)
line: 南朝四百八十寺，多少樓臺煙雨中。
gloss: Of the four hundred and eighty temples of the Southern Dynasties — how many towers and terraces now stand veiled in misty rain?
imagery: A vast Jiangnan riverland seen from afar: faint silhouettes of countless temple towers and tiered pavilions receding into depth, all dissolving into a soft grey-blue curtain of drifting rain-mist (煙雨). The buildings are barely there — ghost-rooflines half-erased, appearing and vanishing as veils of vapor roll across them. Melancholy, layered distance, time blurred into weather.
motion: Sheets of soft mist DRIFT laterally across stacked layers of faint architectural silhouettes, continuously veiling and unveiling them — a tower-roof emerges as a thin curtain thins, then dims back to nothing as the next veil passes (多少…煙雨中). Parallax depth: near mist-sheets slide faster than the far ones, and the distant rooflines fade in and out of legibility. The moving fog that swallows and releases the buildings is the whole motion.
concept: Build 3-4 depth layers of very faint warm-grey temple/pavilion silhouettes (simple tiered-roof SDF shapes), dimmest at the back. Over them scroll independent fBm fog sheets at parallax speeds; couple each silhouette's opacity inversely to the local fog density passing over it, so buildings breathe in and out of view. A barely-there vertical rain texture drifts within the fog. Palette: misted blue-grey ground #2a3340 → pale vapor veils #aab6bf, ghost-ash rooflines #c9d2d8 at low alpha, a faint warm temple-lamp ember #b6884a deep in the haze. Soft, low-contrast, dreamlike; large pale-grey mist expanses double as 留白 for text. Unlike yin-hu-shang (foreground sparkle + a single faint hill), this is layered RECEDING ARCHITECTURE devoured by moving fog — the only built-landscape-dissolving-in-mist scene, whose subjects fade in and out of existence with the weather.

name: yu-jia-ao-qiusi
title: 漁家傲·秋思 (Yújiā Ào · Qiūsī) — Autumn Thoughts (Tune: Pride of Fishermen)
author: 范仲淹 (Fàn Zhòngyān)
line: 塞下秋來風景異，衡陽雁去無留意。長煙落日孤城閉。
gloss: Autumn comes to the frontier and the scene turns strange — the geese head for Hengyang with not a backward glance. Long smoke, a setting sun, the lone walled city shut.
imagery: A bleak northwest garrison at dusk: a single skein of wild geese strung across a cold ochre sky, pulling away southward over a ridge of jagged peaks; a tall straight column of beacon-smoke rising; a huge low sun; and a tiny isolated walled town at the foot of it all, its gate just closing for the night. Vast desolate sky, almost all negative space.
motion: TWO linked motions: a V-skein of small dark goose-glyphs slides steadily across and OFF the frame toward the lower horizon (衡陽雁去無留意 — irreversible departure, never turning back), shrinking as it goes; behind it the beacon-smoke ribbon climbs slowly and the round sun sinks almost imperceptibly. The receding skein is the lead motion — a flock leaving, not arriving.
concept: Deep ember-and-ash dusk sky over a low ragged mountain silhouette on the bottom edge, one tiny dark city-block notch at the base. A formation of ~9-13 chevron goose points in a loose V translates diagonally toward the lower-right horizon on a slow loop, each point scaling down and fading near the vanishing edge, then the skein respawns top-left. A single near-vertical noise-displaced smoke ribbon (amber→grey) rises on the left; a large dim blood-orange sun disc descends on a multi-minute ramp at right. Palette: muted ember-orange sun #ff8c42, dusty ochre sky #d5ae49, steel-violet far plain #4a4660, near-black geese and ramparts #06070c. Maximal 留白 in the high cold sky for text. The collection's only migrating-geese FORMATION and only frontier-garrison scene — distinct from shi-zhi-sai-shang (a static smoke thread + sun, no birds) and du-zuo-jingting (a flock dissolving upward, not a hard V-skein raking off a frontier horizon).

name: jian-jia
title: 蒹葭 (Jiānjiā) — The Reeds (Shijing, 'Airs of Qin')
author: 佚名《詩經·秦風》 (Anonymous, Classic of Poetry)
line: 蒹葭蒼蒼，白露為霜。所謂伊人，在水一方。
gloss: The reeds are lush and grey-green; the white dew has turned to frost. The one I long for is somewhere on the far side of the water.
imagery: A cold autumn marsh at first light. Tall grey-green reeds stand thick along a still expanse of water, their feathered plumes whitened and stiffened where the night's dew has crystallized to frost. Across the water a faint indistinct figure (伊人) seems to stand on the far bank — forever just out of reach, half-dissolved in the river haze. The founding image of Chinese longing-across-water.
motion: The reed plumes SWAY in a slow cold wind — a field of tall vertical reed-strokes bending and rising in a wind-wave that travels across the marsh — while frost-glints kindle and twinkle along their frosted tips (白露為霜) as the low light catches the ice. Far across the water the faint silhouette of 伊人 wavers in the haze, dimming and reappearing but never approaching. Reeds bending in wind + frost sparkling on their tips is the lead motion; the unreachable shimmering figure is the quiet secondary beat.
concept: Lower half: a dense rank of thin vertical reed strands (tapered lines with feathered plume tops) anchored at the bottom, each bending on a phase-shifted sine so a wind-wave visibly sweeps across them. Scatter additive frost-points along the upper plumes that twinkle in/out on per-point phases (dew-turned-frost catching dawn). Mid-frame: a flat still water band with faint horizontal haze. On the far bank a single dim soft humanoid silhouette whose opacity wavers on slow noise — present, never nearer. Palette: cold grey-green reeds #6d8a72 → pale frost-silver tips #e8f0ff, frost glints #ffffff, steel-grey dawn water #9fb0bd, near-black sky #06080c, far figure a faint cool ash #b6cff5. Reeds occupy the lower band; the wide pale upper sky/water is open 留白. The set has draping willow strands (yong-liu); these are upright marsh REEDS bending in a traveling wind with frost on their tips plus a unique unreachable-figure-across-water motif — the only reed-marsh and the only 詩經 source in the collection.

name: yanmen-taishou
title: 雁門太守行 (Yànmén Tàishǒu Xíng) — Song of the Yanmen Governor
author: 李賀 (Lǐ Hè)
line: 黑雲壓城城欲摧，甲光向日金鱗開。
gloss: Black cloud bears down on the city — the walls seem about to crush; armor-light meets the breaking sun, golden scales splitting open.
imagery: A frontier siege at the Wild-Goose Pass: a vast black storm-front of cloud rolling DOWN onto a beleaguered city wall, so heavy the ramparts look ready to buckle — then a knife of sunlight stabs through the gap and catches the defenders' scale-armor, which flares in a rippling sheet of metallic gold (金鱗開 — 'golden scales opening'). The most martial, most chromatically violent scene: crushing dark against a sudden blaze of gold.
motion: The BLACK CLOUD-FRONT descends — a heavy turbulent dark mass rolls down from the top, lowering and bulging toward a fixed city-wall line until it nearly engulfs it (壓城 — the press of cloud is the looming motion); at the moment of maximum press a sun-break tears a horizontal gap and a sweeping band of GOLD specular glints ripples left-to-right across the wall (金鱗開 — armor-scales catching light, igniting in sequence). Then the cloud lifts slightly and the cycle breathes. Descending-cloud-press plus the traveling gold armor-flash are the paired motions.
concept: A low dark crenellated wall-silhouette across the bottom. From the top a churning near-black fBm storm-cloud descends on a slow press-and-lift envelope, its underside bulging toward the wall. At peak press, open a thin bright horizontal sun-gap behind the wall and sweep a band of threshold-gated GOLD specular glints across the rampart's top (each 'scale' igniting in left-to-right sequence, like sun running along armor), then fade as the cloud lifts. Keep the mid-sky (above wall, below cloud) open and dark for text between presses. Palette: crushing storm-black #07080e, city-wall darker mass #04050a, sun-break amber #ffad47, blazing armor-gold scale-glints #f2c960→#fef1d1. The collection's only martial/siege scene and only descending storm-FRONT with a traveling metallic-glint flash — distinct from yu-jia-ao-qiusi's quiet closed city (no storm, no gold) and from every water-glint scene (these glints run along stone armor, not waves).

name: ci-beigu
title: 次北固山下 (Cì Běigù Shān Xià) — Mooring Below Beigu Mountain
author: 王灣 (Wáng Wān)
line: 海日生殘夜，江春入舊年。鄉書何處達，歸雁洛陽邊。
gloss: The sea-sun is born from the dying night; spring on the river breaks into the old year. Where will my letter home arrive? — Carry it, returning geese, to Luoyang's side.
imagery: Daybreak on a wide calm river near the sea: a red sun being BORN straight up out of the water at the edge of night, the dark still draining from the upper sky. Above, a single homing goose flying NORTH (歸雁) carries the traveler's letter toward distant Luoyang. A lone moored boat with one taut sail below. A sunrise — birth, return, the turn of the year — opposite in feeling to the canon's many sunsets.
motion: TWO motions: the red sun-disc RISES (生 — born from the sea), easing upward out of a horizontal water-glow at the bottom while the sky overhead lightens from black to dawn-blue; and a single bright goose-glyph crosses high and away toward the upper edge (歸雁 — a lone bird returning north, the inverse of yu-jia-ao-qiusi's departing skein). The sun's birth from the water is the lead motion; the solitary homing goose is the counter-note above it.
concept: Lower edge: a horizontal sea-glow band where a warm red-gold sun disc eases UPWARD on a slow ramp, its reflection a short shimmering vertical streak on rippling water. The whole sky gradient lightens over the cycle (near-black → dawn indigo → pale rose at the waterline) — night draining as the sun is 'born'. High in the frame, one small luminous goose glyph drifts slowly toward the top corner on a gentle wing-bob, fading at the edge. A faint single sail-triangle sits low and still. Palette: dawn-rose horizon #f6c5be, rising sun #ff8c42→#fef1d1, draining night-indigo zenith #0a1024, pale goose #e8f0ff. Open dawn sky for text. The set's only SUNRISE (sun rising from water, sky brightening — the deliberate inverse of deng-guanque-lou's and mu-jiang-yin's sunsets) and its only single homing/letter-bearing goose flying north.
